title: Senior Director, Technology Portfolio & Governance
raw_jd:
Senior Director, Technology Portfolio & Governance

Mirion Technologies • United States (Remote)

About the job

The Director of Technology Portfolio & Governance is a senior IT leadership role responsible for ensuring technology investments, initiatives, and vendor partnerships are strategically aligned with enterprise objectives. Reporting to the CIO, this role provides enterprise‑wide oversight of IT financial management, procurement and vendor management, business relationship management, project and portfolio management (PMO), and enterprise architecture governance.

This leader establishes and enforces governance frameworks, drives portfolio transparency and value realization, and ensures disciplined execution across the IT organization while enabling agility, innovation, and modernization. The role partners closely with executive leadership to balance strategic investment, operational excellence, and risk management, and serve as a trusted advisor to the CIO.

The Senior Director may have direct supervisory responsibility for a team, including Director-level roles.

Occasional travel of up to 10% may be required.

Key Responsibilities

    Ensure strong business relationship management by translating business needs into clear technology roadmaps and outcomes.
    Provide executive oversight of the PMO, ensuring consistent delivery, portfolio reporting, benefits tracking, and continuous improvement.
    Govern enterprise architecture to ensure solutions align with target architectures, security standards, scalability, and technical debt management.
    Establish and govern enterprise IT frameworks, standards, and decision‑making to ensure alignment with business strategy, value delivery, and risk management.
    Own the end‑to‑end IT portfolio, partnering with executives to prioritize initiatives and balance innovation, operational excellence, and compliance.
    Provide oversight of IT financial management, including budgeting, forecasting, cost transparency, ROI tracking, and value realization.
    Drive financial discipline while enabling strategic investment, modernization, and long‑term technology sustainability.
    Lead IT procurement and vendor management, including sourcing strategy, contract negotiation, performance oversight, and risk management.
    Build strategic supplier partnerships that improve cost efficiency, service quality, and alignment with business goals.
    Lead and develop senior leaders and teams, fostering accountability, collaboration, and serving as a trusted advisor to the CIO and enterprise leadership.

Qualifications And Experience

    Experience: 12+ years of progressive experience in IT leadership, with significant exposure to portfolio management, governance, enterprise architecture, or enterprise IT operations, including 8+ years in senior IT leadership roles with strong people management responsibility in complex, global environments. 
    Education: Bachelor’s degree in information technology, Business, Finance, or a related field
    Technical Expertise: Strong understanding of IT financial management, vendor management, PMO practices, and enterprise architecture concepts. Previous experience launching an Enterprise Architecture program for a relative sized organization, preferred.
    Leadership Skills: Demonstrated experience managing senior leaders and complex, cross‑functional teams, driving infrastructure modernization initiatives, and aligning technology operations with business and organizational priorities in dynamic, multi-region organizations. Proven ability to influence executive stakeholders and drive organizational change.
    Execution & Decision-Making: Proven capability to manage large, complex initiatives, make sound decisions under pressure, and present recommendations to senior leadership.
    Operational Excellence: Track record of driving process improvement, operational efficiency, governance discipline, and high-quality outcomes.
    Communication & Management: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, strong organizational abilities, and the capacity to manage multiple priorities concurrently.
    Languages: Proficiency in French and/or German is preferred.
    Talent Strategy: Demonstrated ability to build, develop, and sustain high-performing teams through effective workforce planning, engagement, and leadership development.

score.skills_rationale: Strong alignment with candidate's IT governance, enterprise architecture, PMO, and portfolio management background. Candidate has deep experience in financial management, vendor management, and strategic technology leadership across multiple organizations including federal and enterprise environments.
score.scope_rationale: The Senior Director role aligns well with candidate's executive experience as CEO/CTO and current senior architect role. However, the emphasis on managing Director-level direct reports and 12+ years IT leadership requirement suggests this may be slightly above candidate's current positioning, though within reach given consulting and architecture leadership experience.
score.pay_rationale: No salary range provided in JD, making pay alignment impossible to assess. Senior Director roles at enterprise companies typically range $180K-$250K+ which could align with candidate's W2 targets.
score.raw_analysis: This is a legitimate Senior Director role at an established technology company with clear governance and portfolio management responsibilities that align well with the candidate's background. The role combines strategic IT leadership, financial oversight, and enterprise architecture - all areas where the candidate has demonstrated expertise. While the seniority level is high, the candidate's diverse leadership experience across federal, university, and private sector environments positions them competitively. The lack of salary transparency is the main concern for targeting.

cover_letters.content:
Dear Hiring Manager,

As a technology leader with 25+ years delivering enterprise-scale IT governance, portfolio management, and strategic architecture initiatives, I am writing to express my interest in the Senior Director, Technology Portfolio & Governance role at Mirion Technologies. My track record leading complex ERP transformations, establishing data governance frameworks, and driving cross-functional technology initiatives across federal, university, and private sector environments directly aligns with your requirements for strategic IT oversight and execution.

Currently serving as Senior Data & Analytics Architect for the Department of Veterans Affairs' $200M+ Financial Management Business Transformation program, I provide hands-on leadership across enterprise architecture, vendor management, and portfolio delivery for one of the government's largest ERP modernizations. At Howard University, I played a pivotal role stabilizing a multi-million-dollar Workday implementation during executive disruption, stepping in as one of three cross-functional leaders to drive the program to successful deployment while establishing enterprise-wide data governance policies. These experiences demonstrate my ability to translate business needs into technology roadmaps, manage complex stakeholder relationships, and deliver results in high-pressure, ambiguous environments.

My background encompasses the full spectrum of responsibilities outlined in your role: I've designed and implemented enterprise architectures integrating legacy systems with modern platforms, led procurement and vendor optimization initiatives that generated $312K+ in annual savings, and established governance frameworks that improved data quality and audit readiness. As a former CEO/CTO and experienced consultant, I bring both strategic vision and operational execution capabilities, with proven success mentoring technical teams and influencing executive stakeholders across diverse organizational contexts.

I am drawn to Mirion Technologies' mission-critical work in radiation detection and measurement, and I'm confident my expertise in balancing innovation with operational excellence would contribute significantly to your technology portfolio strategy. I welcome the opportunity to discuss how my leadership experience and technical depth can drive value for your IT organization and broader enterprise objectives.

Sincerely,
Salil Maniktahla

Dear Hiring Manager,

As organizations navigate increasingly complex technology landscapes, the challenge of aligning IT investments with strategic business outcomes while maintaining operational excellence has never been more critical. Your Senior Director role resonates strongly with challenges I've successfully addressed throughout my 25+ years leading technology initiatives across federal, enterprise, and consulting environments—particularly in establishing governance frameworks that enable both innovation and disciplined execution.

My experience leading the data architecture and reporting workstreams for the Department of Veterans Affairs' Financial Management Business Transformation program directly aligns with your need for enterprise-wide IT oversight. In this role, I've coordinated across finance, acquisition, and IT stakeholders to deliver complex ERP integrations while establishing data validation and reconciliation processes that improved audit readiness—experience that translates well to governing technology portfolios and ensuring value realization. Similarly, during Howard University's multi-million-dollar Workday ERP implementation, I stepped into a critical cross-functional leadership role during executive disruption, helping stabilize delivery and drive the program to successful deployment while establishing enterprise BI architecture and data governance policies that improved system reliability and decision-making capabilities.

What particularly draws me to this opportunity is the combination of strategic portfolio oversight with hands-on problem-solving. Whether designing resilient data pipelines, establishing governance frameworks during system transitions, or mentoring technical teams through complex delivery challenges, I've consistently bridged the gap between infrastructure complexity and business needs. My background spanning enterprise architecture, financial management, vendor optimization (including saving $312K annually through strategic vendor management at MicroStrategy), and cross-functional leadership positions me well to serve as the trusted advisor to your CIO while building the governance discipline and strategic partnerships that enable sustainable technology growth.

I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my experience in establishing governance frameworks, managing complex technology initiatives, and driving organizational alignment can contribute to Mirion Technologies' strategic objectives.

Sincerely,
Salil Maniktahla

I'm writing to express my interest in the Senior Director, Technology Portfolio & Governance role at Mirion Technologies. With 25+ years of IT leadership experience spanning federal ERP implementations, enterprise architecture, and cross-functional team leadership, I bring the strategic oversight and execution discipline this role demands. Currently leading data architecture for the VA's $2B+ FMBT program, I've designed enterprise data pipelines, established governance frameworks, and coordinated delivery across Finance, IT, and acquisition stakeholders—directly aligning with your portfolio management and business relationship requirements.

My background uniquely combines the technical depth and financial oversight you're seeking: I drove Howard University's multi-million-dollar Workday ERP implementation as one of three cross-functional leaders during executive disruption, stabilized delivery timelines, and established data governance policies that improved audit readiness. Earlier, as BI Senior Manager at VitalSpring, I identified process bottlenecks through analytics that saved $70K+ in under six months, and at MicroStrategy, I led vendor optimization efforts saving ~$312K annually. This blend of enterprise architecture governance, IT financial management, and hands-on problem-solving positions me to drive portfolio transparency and value realization while enabling the strategic modernization initiatives Mirion requires.

tailored_resume:
SALIL MANIKTAHLA
Springfield, VA  ·  571-215-8218  ·  salil.maniktahla@gmail.com  ·  linkedin.com/in/salilmaniktahla

SUMMARY
Senior technology leader with 25+ years of progressive experience in IT portfolio management, governance, and enterprise architecture across federal, academic, and private sector environments. Currently leading data architecture and technology integration for the Department of Veterans Affairs' Financial Management Business Transformation (FMBT) program, a large-scale ERP modernization initiative requiring disciplined portfolio oversight, vendor coordination, and enterprise architecture governance.

Proven track record of translating business needs into technology roadmaps, establishing governance frameworks, and driving value realization through strategic IT investments. Experienced in managing complex, cross-functional teams and serving as trusted advisor to C-level executives during critical technology transformations, including multi-million-dollar ERP implementations and enterprise modernization initiatives.

CORE CAPABILITIES
IT Portfolio & Financial Management
    • Portfolio prioritization, ROI tracking, and value realization across enterprise technology investments
    • IT budgeting, forecasting, cost transparency, and financial discipline
    • Strategic technology roadmaps aligned with business objectives

Enterprise Architecture & Governance
    • Enterprise architecture governance, target architecture definition, and technical debt management
    • Technology standards, frameworks, and decision-making processes
    • Solution alignment with security standards, scalability, and compliance requirements

Vendor Management & Procurement
    • IT procurement strategy, contract negotiation, and supplier performance oversight
    • Strategic vendor partnerships for cost efficiency and service quality improvement
    • Risk management across vendor relationships and technology investments

Project & Portfolio Management (PMO)
    • Executive PMO oversight with consistent delivery, portfolio reporting, and benefits tracking
    • Large-scale technology initiative management and organizational change leadership
    • Process improvement and operational excellence across IT operations

Leadership & Business Relationship Management
    • Senior team leadership including Director-level roles and cross-functional coordination
    • Executive stakeholder influence and trusted advisor relationships
    • Business needs translation into clear technology outcomes and strategic alignment

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

Senior Data & Analytics Architect (Contractor)
U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs – FMBT	Sep 2021 – Present
    • Lead technology portfolio oversight for Financial Management Business Transformation, a large-scale ERP modernization initiative requiring enterprise architecture governance and vendor coordination
    • Establish governance frameworks for data integration across legacy systems and ERP platforms, ensuring alignment with target architectures and compliance requirements
    • Drive financial discipline through cost transparency, ROI tracking, and value realization across technology investments supporting CFO-level visibility and decision-making
    • Coordinate vendor relationships and procurement activities across multiple technology suppliers, ensuring performance oversight and risk management
    • Provide executive reporting on portfolio status, benefits tracking, and continuous improvement initiatives
    • Partner with finance, acquisition, and IT stakeholders to balance strategic investment with operational excellence

Business Intelligence Analyst – Finance
Howard University	Apr 2020 – Jul 2024
    • Served as one of three key technology leaders driving multi-million-dollar Workday ERP implementation during executive disruption, demonstrating ability to manage complex initiatives and make sound decisions under pressure
    • Established enterprise governance frameworks and data architecture standards, ensuring solution alignment with business strategy and technical requirements
    • Led strategic technology initiatives as trusted advisor to CFO and Deputy CFO, translating business needs into clear technology roadmaps and outcomes
    • Drove IT financial management including budgeting, forecasting, and value realization across enterprise systems
    • Managed vendor relationships and procurement activities for enterprise technology solutions
    • Built and developed cross-functional teams across Finance, HR, and IT organizations

Co-Founder / CEO & Technical Director
Urban Evolution	Apr 2010 – Apr 2020
    • Served as CEO, CTO, and CIO, owning end-to-end technology portfolio management and strategic business alignment across multi-location operation
    • Established governance frameworks, technology standards, and operational processes supporting business growth and modernization
    • Led IT financial management including budgeting, vendor management, and ROI optimization across all technology investments
    • Built and managed senior leadership team, fostering accountability and collaboration across business and technical operations
    • Drove infrastructure modernization initiatives and technology operations alignment with organizational priorities

BI Partner / Strategist (Independent Consultant)
Multiple Clients	Jul 2016 – Mar 2020
    • Provided portfolio management and governance consulting across multiple enterprise clients, establishing frameworks aligned with business strategy and value delivery
    • Led technology initiative oversight with focus on governance discipline, process improvement, and operational excellence
    • Mentored senior technology leaders and managed complex, cross-functional teams across global environments
    • Evaluated and improved enterprise systems for performance, scalability, and alignment with target architectures

VitalSpring Technologies
BI Senior Manager	May 2008 – Jul 2016
    • Served as Product Manager for enterprise BI/analytics portfolio, driving strategic alignment and value realization
    • Established governance frameworks and PMO practices across technology initiatives, delivering measurable business outcomes including $70K+ cost savings
    • Led Agile delivery methodologies and managed senior technical teams across complex, multi-region organization
    • Drove vendor management and procurement activities for analytics platforms and enterprise solutions

EARLIER EXPERIENCE

Senior BI Consultant – Claraview (Teradata), 2006–2008
    • Technical architect for 25,000-user enterprise platform (Lowe's); established KPIs and governance frameworks for Fortune 100 client

BI Consultant – Kinetic Networks, 2005–2006
    • Managed technology portfolios for Google, Yahoo, Wells Fargo; drove strategic alignment and value delivery

IT Specialist – U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, 2002–2005
    • Led team of 6 engineers; defined IT strategy and governance for National Center for Environmental Economics

Operations Manager – MicroStrategy, 1999–2001
    • Managed senior engineering teams; delivered enterprise analytics solutions; achieved $312K annual cost optimization through vendor management

SELECTED SYSTEMS & INNOVATION
    • Designed enterprise LLM/RAG platform with role-based governance and audit compliance for secure knowledge management
    • Established containerized enterprise architecture supporting distributed systems and operational excellence
    • Built integrated technology platforms combining data governance, automation, and monitoring aligned with enterprise standards
    • Operate advanced technology infrastructure (10GbE backbone, 100TB+ storage) demonstrating hands-on technical leadership

TECHNICAL SKILLS
Enterprise Architecture: Solution architecture, target architecture definition, technical debt management
IT Financial Management: Portfolio budgeting, ROI tracking, cost optimization, value realization
PMO & Governance: Project portfolio oversight, benefits tracking, process improvement
Data & Analytics: SQL, Python, ETL/ELT, Data Modeling, Enterprise Data Architecture
Platforms: Tableau, Power BI, MicroStrategy, Azure, Docker, Linux
Vendor Management: Procurement strategy, contract negotiation, supplier performance management

CERTIFICATIONS & EDUCATION
B.A., Washington University in St. Louis
CISSP  ·  Certified Scrum Master  ·  Tableau Certified Professional  ·  MicroStrategy Certified Developer
